content.boundary: Applies to villages under 2,000 inhabitants currently operating full-cycle primary schools with declining upper-grade enrollment; does not apply to villages that have already lost lower-grade viability (zero-enrollment cliff cases).
content.change_lever: Legally restructure school cycles so that lower grades (1-4) remain village-based while upper grades are consolidated into a regional ISCED2-type school, following the structural (not political-reversal) logic of Poland's 1999 gimnazjum reform rather than its 2017 reversal.
content.name: Structural grade-splitting: retain lower grades locally, centralize upper grades (Polish-pattern reorganization)
content.system_problem: Small village schools cannot sustain full-cycle (grades 1-8/1-6) staffing quality (subject specialization, teacher recruitment) under current enrollment and funding-formula pressure, yet full closure removes the youngest children's local access entirely, imposing the longest relative commute burden on the age group least able to bear it.
